answers.where: Yellow Backrooms corridor facing a deeper non-Euclidean room aperture with denser native room surfaces, contact-shadowed jambs, floor/wall/ceiling panels, fluorescent spill, copied mark, conduit, threshold, and landmark column
answers.action: Use the copied mark as evidence, find the missing handle, and test the locked door
answers.response: The mark appears inside the impossible room; the runtime now shades 450 native nt_gfx overlay vertices with denser surface alpha and a separate aperture-occluder contact-shadow layer
answers.reward: The player learns the space can be tested and later stabilized by fitting the found handle
answers.game_look: Native 3D liminal horror view with a larger-inside portal room, stained wallpaper, carpet seams, ceiling grid, shadowed aperture, minimal journal UI, and texture-backed material geometry

issues.text: Denser surfaces and aperture contact shadows reduce the transparent overlay feel, but the room is still not a fully integrated physically lit 3D/render-target space
The non-Euclidean hook is stronger, but the screenshot still needs a more distinctive high-quality horror look to stand out among many Backrooms games
